heading: Special rules for top-heavy plans
label: 416 Special rules for top-heavy plans
notes: Inflation Adjusted Items for Certain YearsFor inflation adjustment of certain items in this section, see Internal Revenue Notices listed in a table under .section 401 of this title

References in TextThe Federal Insurance Contributions Act, referred to in subsec. (e), is , §§ 3101, 3102, 3111, 3112, 3121 to 3128, , which is classified generally to chapter 21 (§ 3101 et seq.) of this title. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see and Tables.act Aug. 16, 1954, ch. 73668A Stat. 415section 3128 of this titleThe Social Security Act, referred to in subsec. (e), is , . Amendments2022—Subsec. (c)(2)(C). , added subpar. (C).Pub. L. 117–328, § 310(a)Subsec. (g)(4)(H). , inserted “Such term shall not include a plan solely because such plan does not provide nonelective or matching contributions to employees described in section 401(k)(15)(B)(i).” before “If, but” in concluding provisions.Pub. L. 117–328, § 125(e), substituted “arrangements or plans” for “arrangements” in heading and “and matching contributions with respect to which the requirements of paragraph (11), (12), or (13) of section 401(m) are met, or” for “, and” in cl. (i), added cl. (ii), and struck out former cl. (ii) which read as follows: “matching contributions with respect to which the requirements of section 401(m)(11) or 401(m)(12) are met.”Pub. L. 117–328, § 121(c)2006—Subsec. (g)(4)(H)(i). , inserted “or 401(k)(13)” after “401(k)(12)”.Pub. L. 109–280, § 902(c)(1)Subsec. (g)(4)(H)(ii). , inserted “or 401(m)(12)” after “401(m)(11)”.Pub. L. 109–280, § 902(c)(2)2004—Subsec. (i)(1)(A). substituted “In the case of plan years” for “in the case of plan years” in concluding provisions.Pub. L. 108–3112002—Subsec. (c)(1)(C)(iii). , substituted “Exception for plan under which no key employee (or former key employee) benefits for plan year” for “Exception for frozen plan” in heading.Pub. L. 107–147, § 411(k)(1)Subsec. (g)(3)(B). , substituted “severance from employment” for “separation from service”.Pub. L. 107–147, § 411(k)(2)2001—Subsec. (c)(1)(C)(i). , substituted “clause (ii) or (iii)” for “clause (ii)”.Pub. L. 107–16, § 613(e)(A)Subsec. (c)(1)(C)(iii). , added cl. (iii).Pub. L. 107–16, § 613(e)(B)Subsec. (c)(2)(A). , inserted at end “Employer matching contributions (as defined in section 401(m)(4)(A)) shall be taken into account for purposes of this subparagraph (and any reduction under this sentence shall not be taken into account in determining whether section 401(k)(4)(A) applies).”Pub. L. 107–16, § 613(b)Subsec. (g)(3). , amended heading and text of par. (3) generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “For purposes of determining—Pub. L. 107–16, § 613(c)(1)“(A) the present value of the cumulative accrued benefit for any employee, or“(B) the amount of the account of any employee,such present value or amount shall be increased by the aggregate distributions made with respect to such employee under the plan during the 5-year period ending on the determination date. The preceding sentence shall also apply to distributions under a terminated plan which if it had not been terminated would have been required to be included in an aggregation group.”Subsec. (g)(4)(E). , in heading substituted “last year before determination date” for “last 5 years” and in text substituted “1-year period” for “5-year period”.Pub. L. 107–16, § 613(c)(2)Subsec. (g)(4)(H). , added subpar. (H).Pub. L. 107–16, § 613(d)Subsec. (i)(1)(A). , in concluding provisions, substituted “in the case of plan years beginning after , the $130,000 amount in clause (i) shall be adjusted at the same time and in the same manner as under section 415(d), except that the base period shall be the calendar quarter beginning , and any increase under this sentence which is not a multiple of $5,000 shall be rounded to the next lower multiple of $5,000.” for “For purposes of clause (ii), if 2 employees have the same interest in the employer, the employee having greater annual compensation from the employer shall be treated as having a larger interest.”Pub. L. 107–16, § 613(a)(1)(D)December 31, 2002July 1, 2001, struck out “or any of the 4 preceding plan years” after “plan year” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 107–16, § 613(a)(1)(A)Subsec. (i)(1)(A)(i). , § 613(a)(1)(B), added cl. (i) and struck out former cl. (i) which read as follows: “an officer of the employer having an annual compensation greater than 50 percent of the amount in effect under section 415(b)(1)(A) for any such plan year,”.Pub. (ii) which read as follows: “1 of the 10 employees having annual compensation from the employer of more than the limitation in effect under section 415(c)(1)(A) and owning (or considered as owning within the meaning of section 318) the largest interests in the employer,”.Pub. (g)(4)(G). , added subpar. (G).Pub. L. 104–188, § 1421(b)(7)Subsec. (h). , struck out subsec. (h) which related to adjustments in section 415 limits for top-heavy plans.Pub. (i)(1)(A). , inserted at end “For purposes of determining the number of officers taken into account under clause (i), employees described in section 414(q)(8) shall be excluded.”Pub. L. 100–647, § 1011(i)(4)(B)Subsec. (i)(1)(A)(i). , substituted “50” for “150” and “415(b)(1)(A)” for “415(c)(1)(A)”.Pub. L. 100–647, § 1011(d)(8)Subsec. (i)(1)(D). , added subpar. (D).Pub. L. 100–647, § 1011(j)(3)(A)1986—Subsec. (a)(3). , struck out par. (3) which read as follows: “the limitation on compensation requirement of subsection (d).”Pub. L. 99–514, § 1106(d)(3)(A)Subsec. (c)(2)(B)(ii), (iii). , redesignated cl. (iii) as (ii) and struck out former cl. (ii) which read as follows: “.—The determination referred to in clause (i) shall be determined for each key employee by dividing the contributions for such employee by so much of his total compensation for the year as does not exceed $200,000.”Pub. L. 99–514, § 1106(d)(3)(B)(ii)Determination of percentageSubsec. (d). , repealed subsec. (d) which provided for a $200,000 limitation on the amount of annual compensation of each employee taken into account.Pub. L. 99–514, § 1106(d)(3)(B)(i)Subsec. (g)(4)(E). , amended subpar. (E) generally. Prior to amendment, subpar. (E) read as follows: “If any individual has not received any compensation from any employer maintaining the plan (other than benefits under the plan) at any time during the 5-year period ending on the determination date, any accrued benefit for such individual (and the account of such individual) shall not be taken into account.”Pub. L. 99–514, § 1852(d)(2)Subsec. (g)(4)(F). , added subpar. (F).Pub. L. 99–514, § 1118(a)Subsec. (i)(1)(A). , inserted at end “Such term shall not include any officer or employee of an entity referred to in section 414(d) (relating to governmental plans).”Pub. L. 99–514, § 1852(d)(1)1984—Subsec. (c)(2)(C). , struck out subpar. (C) which provided that for purposes of this paragraph, any employer contribution attributable to a salary reduction or similar arrangement shall not be taken into account.Pub. L. 98–369, § 524(c)(1)Subsec. (d)(2). , inserted “at the same time and”.Pub. L. 98–369, § 713(f)(5)(A)Subsec. (f). , substituted “required” for “require”.Pub. L. 98–369, § 713(f)(6)(A)Subsec. (g)(3). , inserted at end “The preceding sentence shall also apply to distributions under a terminated plan which if it had not been terminated would have been required to be included in an aggregation group.”Pub. L. 98–369, § 713(f)(4)Subsec. (g)(4)(E). , added subpar. (E).Pub. L. 98–369, § 524(b)(1)Subsec. (i)(1)(A). , (C), substituted in provisions preceding cl. (i) “an employee” for “any participant in an employer plan” and inserted at end thereof provision for treatment of an employee with the greater annual compensation as having a larger interest in the employer where, for purposes of cl. (ii), 2 employees have the same interest in the employer.Pub. L. 98–369, § 713(f)(1)(A)Subsec. (i)(1)(A)(i). , inserted “having an annual compensation greater than 150 percent of the amount in effect under section 415(c)(1)(A) for any plan year”.Pub. L. 98–369, § 524(a)(1)Subsec. (i)(1)(A)(ii). , required a key employee to have annual compensation from the employer of more than the limitation in effect under section 415(c)(1)(A).Pub. L. 98–369, § 713(f)(1)(B)Subsec. (i)(1)(B)(iii). , substituted subparagraph “(A)(ii)” for “(A)(ii)(II)”.Pub. L. 98–369, § 713(f)(6)(B)Subsec. (i)(1)(C). , substituted in heading “ownership in the employer” for “5-percent or 1-percent owners”.Pub. L. 98–369, § 713(f)(1)(A)

children.text: the vesting requirements of subsection (b), and
the minimum benefit requirements of subsection (c).

children.heading: 3-year vesting
children.label: A 3-year vesting
children.text: A plan satisfies the requirements of this subparagraph if an employee who has completed at least 3 years of service with the employer or employers maintaining the plan has a nonforfeitable right to 100 percent of his accrued benefit derived from employer contributions.

children.heading: 6-year graded vesting
children.label: B 6-year graded vesting
children.text: A plan satisfies the requirements of this subparagraph if an employee has a nonforfeitable right to a percentage of his accrued benefit derived from employer contributions determined under the following table:Years of serviceThe nonforfeitable percentage is:2203404605806 or more100

children.heading: Certain rules made applicable
children.label: 2 Certain rules made applicable
children.text: Except to the extent inconsistent with the provisions of this subsection, the rules of section 411 shall apply for purposes of this subsection.

children.heading: Plan must provide minimum benefits
children.label: c Plan must provide minimum benefits

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A defined benefit plan meets the requirements of this subsection if the accrued benefit derived from employer contributions of each participant who is a non-key employee, when expressed as an annual retirement benefit, is not less than the applicable percentage of the participant’s average compensation for years in the testing period.

children.label: i
children.text: 2 percent multiplied by the number of years of service with the employer, or

children.label: ii
children.text: 20 percent.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Except as provided in clause (ii) or (iii), years of service shall be determined under the rules of paragraphs (4), (5), and (6) of section 411(a).

children.heading: Exception for years during which plan was not top-heavy
children.label: ii Exception for years during which plan was not top-heavy
I
children.text: the plan was not a top-heavy plan for any plan year ending during such year of service, or

children.label: II
children.text: such year of service was completed in a plan year beginning before .January 1, 1984

children.heading: Exception for plan under which no key employee (or former key employee) benefits for plan year
children.label: iii Exception for plan under which no key employee (or former key employee) benefits for plan year
children.text: For purposes of determining an employee’s years of service with the employer, any service with the employer shall be disregarded to the extent that such service occurs during a plan year when the plan benefits (within the meaning of section 410(b)) no key employee or former key employee.

children.heading: Average compensation for high 5 years
children.label: D Average compensation for high 5 years

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: A participant’s testing period shall be the period of consecutive years (not exceeding 5) during which the participant had the greatest aggregate compensation from the employer.

children.heading: Year must be included in year of service
children.label: ii Year must be included in year of service
children.text: The years taken into account under clause (i) shall be properly adjusted for years not included in a year of service.

children.heading: Certain years not taken into account
children.label: iii Certain years not taken into account
I
children.text: such year ends in a plan year beginning before , orJanuary 1, 1984

children.label: II
children.text: such year begins after the close of the last year in which the plan was a top-heavy plan.

children.heading: Annual retirement benefit
children.label: E Annual retirement benefit
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “annual retirement benefit” means a benefit payable annually in the form of a single life annuity (with no ancillary benefits) beginning at the normal retirement age under the plan.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A defined contribution plan meets the requirements of the subsection if the employer contribution for the year for each participant who is a non-key employee is not less than 3 percent of such participant’s compensation (within the meaning of section 415). Employer matching contributions (as defined in section 401(m)(4)(A)) shall be taken into account for purposes of this subparagraph (and any reduction under this sentence shall not be taken into account in determining whether section 401(k)(4)(A) applies).

children.heading: Special rule where maximum contribution less than 3 percent
children.label: B Special rule where maximum contribution less than 3 percent

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The percentage referred to in subparagraph (A) for any year shall not exceed the percentage at which contributions are made (or required to be made) under the plan for the year for the key employee for whom such percentage is the highest for the year.

children.label: I
children.text: For purposes of this subparagraph, all defined contribution plans required to be included in an aggregation group under subsection (g)(2)(A)(i) shall be treated as one plan.

children.label: II
children.text: This subparagraph shall not apply to any plan required to be included in an aggregation group if such plan enables a defined benefit plan required to be included in such group to meet the requirements of section 401(a)(4) or 410.

children.heading: Application to employees not meeting age and service requirements
children.label: C Application to employees not meeting age and service requirements
children.text: Any employees not meeting the age or service requirements of section 410(a)(1) (without regard to subparagraph (B) thereof) may be excluded from consideration in determining whether any plan of the employer meets the requirements of subparagraphs (A) and (B).

children.heading: Repealed. , , ]Pub. L. 99–514, title XI, § 1106(d)(3)(B)(i)100 Stat. 2424Oct. 22, 1986
children.label: d Repealed. , , ]Pub. L. 99–514, title XI, § 1106(d)(3)(B)(i)100 Stat. 2424Oct. 22, 1986

children.heading: Plan must meet requirements without taking into account social security and similar contributions and benefits
children.label: e Plan must meet requirements without taking into account social security and similar contributions and benefits
children.text: A top-heavy plan shall not be treated as meeting the requirement of subsection (b) or (c) unless such plan meets such requirement without taking into account contributions or benefits under chapter 2 (relating to tax on self-employment income), chapter 21 (relating to Federal Insurance Contributions Act), title II of the Social Security Act, or any other Federal or State law.

children.heading: Coordination where employer has 2 or more plans
children.label: f Coordination where employer has 2 or more plans
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe such regulations as may be necessary or appropriate to carry out the purposes of this section where the employer has 2 or more plans including (but not limited to) regulations to prevent inappropriate omissions or required duplication of minimum benefits or contributions.

children.heading: Plans not required to be aggregated
children.label: A Plans not required to be aggregated
i
children.text: any defined benefit plan if, as of the determination date, the present value of the cumulative accrued benefits under the plan for key employees exceeds 60 percent of the present value of the cumulative accrued benefits under the plan for all employees, and

children.label: ii
children.text: any defined contribution plan if, as of the determination date, the aggregate of the accounts of key employees under the plan exceeds 60 percent of the aggregate of the accounts of all employees under such plan.

children.heading: Aggregated plans
children.label: B Aggregated plans
children.text: Each plan of an employer required to be included in an aggregation group shall be treated as a top-heavy plan if such group is a top-heavy group.

children.label: I
children.text: each plan of the employer in which a key employee is a participant, and

children.label: II
children.text: each other plan of the employer which enables any plan described in subclause (I) to meet the requirements of section 401(a)(4) or 410.

children.heading: Permissive aggregation
children.label: ii Permissive aggregation
children.text: The employer may treat any plan not required to be included in an aggregation group under clause (i) as being part of such group if such group would continue to meet the requirements of sections 401(a)(4) and 410 with such plan being taken into account.

children.label: I
children.text: the present value of the cumulative accrued benefits for key employees under all defined benefit plans included in such group, and

children.label: II
children.text: the aggregate of the accounts of key employees under all defined contribution plans included in such group,

children.label: ii
children.text: exceeds 60 percent of a similar sum determined for all employees.

children.heading: Distributions during last year before determination date taken into account
children.label: 3 Distributions during last year before determination date taken into account
i
children.text: the present value of the cumulative accrued benefit for any employee, or

children.label: ii
children.text: the amount of the account of any employee,

children.heading: 5-year period in case of in-service distribution
children.label: B 5-year period in case of in-service distribution
children.text: In the case of any distribution made for a reason other than severance from employment, death, or disability, subparagraph (A) shall be applied by substituting “5-year period” for “1-year period”.

children.heading: Rollover contributions to plan not taken into account
children.label: A Rollover contributions to plan not taken into account
children.text: Except to the extent provided in regulations, any rollover contribution (or similar transfer) initiated by the employee and made after , to a plan shall not be taken into account with respect to the transferee plan for purposes of determining whether such plan is a top-heavy plan (or whether any aggregation group which includes such plan is a top-heavy group).December 31, 1983

children.heading: Benefits not taken into account if employee ceases to be key employee
children.label: B Benefits not taken into account if employee ceases to be key employee
children.text: If any individual is a non-key employee with respect to any plan for any plan year, but such individual was a key employee with respect to such plan for any prior plan year, any accrued benefit for such employee (and the account of such employee) shall not be taken into account.

children.label: i
children.text: the last day of the preceding plan year, or

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of the first plan year of any plan, the last day of such plan year.

children.heading: Years
children.label: D Years
children.text: To the extent provided in regulations, this section shall be applied on the basis of any year specified in such regulations in lieu of plan years.

children.heading: Benefits not taken into account if employee not employed for last year before determination date
children.label: E Benefits not taken into account if employee not employed for last year before determination date
children.text: If any individual has not performed services for the employer maintaining the plan at any time during the 1-year period ending on the determination date, any accrued benefit for such individual (and the account of such individual) shall not be taken into account.

children.heading: Accrued benefits treated as accruing ratably
children.label: F Accrued benefits treated as accruing ratably
i
children.text: under the method which is used for accrual purposes for all plans of the employer, or

children.label: ii
children.text: if there is no method described in clause (i), as if such benefit accrued not more rapidly than the slowest accrual rate permitted under section 411(b)(1)(C).

children.heading: Simple retirement accounts
children.label: G Simple retirement accounts
children.text: The term “top-heavy plan” shall not include a simple retirement account under section 408(p).

children.heading: Cash or deferred arrangements or plans using alternative methods of meeting nondiscrimination requirements
children.label: H Cash or deferred arrangements or plans using alternative methods of meeting nondiscrimination requirements
i
children.text: a cash or deferred arrangement which meets the requirements of section 401(k)(12) or 401(k)(13) and matching contributions with respect to which the requirements of paragraph (11), (12), or (13) of section 401(m) are met, or

children.label: ii
children.text: a starter 401(k) deferral-only arrangement described in section 401(k)(16)(B) or a safe harbor deferral-only plan described in section 403(b)(16).

children.label: i
children.text: an officer of the employer having an annual compensation greater than $130,000,

children.label: ii
children.text: a 5-percent owner of the employer, or

children.label: iii
children.text: a 1-percent owner of the employer having an annual compensation from the employer of more than $150,000.

children.label: I
children.text: if the employer is a corporation, any person who owns (or is considered as owning within the meaning of section 318) more than 5 percent of the outstanding stock of the corporation or stock possessing more than 5 percent of the total combined voting power of all stock of the corporation, or

children.label: II
children.text: if the employer is not a corporation, any person who owns more than 5 percent of the capital or profits interest in the employer.

children.heading: 1-percent owner
children.label: ii 1-percent owner
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “1-percent owner” means any person who would be described in clause (i) if “1 percent” were substituted for “5 percent” each place it appears in clause (i).

children.label: I
children.text: subparagraph (C) of section 318(a)(2) shall be applied by substituting “5 percent” for “50 percent”, and

children.label: II
children.text: in the case of any employer which is not a corporation, ownership in such employer shall be determined in accordance with regulations prescribed by the Secretary which shall be based on principles similar to the principles of section 318 (as modified by subclause (I)).

children.heading: Aggregation rules do not apply for purposes of determining ownership in the employer
children.label: C Aggregation rules do not apply for purposes of determining ownership in the employer
children.text: The rules of subsections (b), (c), and (m) of section 414 shall not apply for purposes of determining ownership in the employer.

children.heading: Compensation
children.label: D Compensation
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “compensation” has the meaning given such term by section 414(q)(4).

children.heading: Non-key employee
children.label: 2 Non-key employee
children.text: The term “non-key employee” means any employee who is not a key employee.

children.label: A
children.text: such individual shall be treated as an employee, and

children.label: B
children.text: such individual’s earned income (within the meaning of section 401(c)(2)) shall be treated as compensation.

children.heading: Treatment of employees covered by collective bargaining agreements
children.label: 4 Treatment of employees covered by collective bargaining agreements
children.text: The requirements of subsections (b), (c), and (d) shall not apply with respect to any employee included in a unit of employees covered by an agreement which the Secretary of Labor finds to be a collective bargaining agreement between employee representatives and 1 or more employers if there is evidence that retirement benefits were the subject of good faith bargaining between such employee representatives and such employer or employers.

children.heading: Treatment of beneficiaries
children.label: 5 Treatment of beneficiaries
children.text: The terms “employee” and “key employee” include their beneficiaries.

children.heading: Treatment as defined contribution plans
children.label: A Treatment as defined contribution plans
children.text: A simplified employee pension shall be treated as a defined contribution plan.

children.heading: Election to have determinations based on employer contributions
children.label: B Election to have determinations based on employer contributions
children.text: In the case of a simplified employee pension, at the election of the employer, paragraphs (1)(A)(ii) and (2)(B) of subsection (g) shall be applied by taking into account aggregate employer contributions in lieu of the aggregate of the accounts of employees.
